name: Camorritos Trail
name_original: Tres Huecos de Cercedilla — Sierra de Guadarrama
description: Tackle the difficult Camorritos Trail, a 15.9-mile mountain biking route in Sierra de Guadarrama with 2029 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Cercedilla are situated within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, offering a diverse landscape for outdoor activities. The region is characterized by extensive Scots pine forests, granite and gneiss geology, and significant elevation changes. Routes often traverse through the Fuenfría Valley, featuring a network of trails and natural features like the Roman Road and various peaks. This area provides a range of terrain from accessible paths to challenging singletracks. Best mountain bike trails around Cercedilla The most popular mountain bike trail is Cercedilla to Puerto de Cotos circular circular route via Valle de la Fuentfría — Guadarrama National Park, a 29.1 miles (46.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 25 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the Fuenfría Valley and offers extensive singletrack networks within the National Park. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Cercedilla to Segovia via La Fuenfría — Sierra de Guadarrama, a difficult 25.0 miles (40.2 km) path. This route connects Cercedilla to the historic town of Segovia, passing through the scenic Sierra de Guadarrama. Local mountain bikers also love the Parque Nacional de Guadarrama-Puerto Navacerrada-Pinares de la Barranca – circular desde Cercedilla por senda de la Tubería, a 15.7 miles (25.3 km) trail leading through the Guadarrama National Park and towards Puerto de Navacerrada, often completed in about 2 hours 45 minutes. Mountain biking around Cercedilla is defined by Scots pine forests, granite formations, and varied singletrack networks. The region offers options for different ability levels, from accessible paths to challenging climbs. The routes in Cercedilla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 140 reviews. More than 2000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Cercedilla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Cercedilla?
A: Cercedilla offers a wide range of mountain biking opportunities, with over 35 trails available. These routes cater to various skill levels, from accessible paths to challenging singletracks.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Cercedilla?
A: Yes, while many routes are challenging, there are a few trails suitable for beginners. You can find 2 easy-rated mountain bike routes around Cercedilla, offering a gentler introduction to the area's beautiful landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Cercedilla?
A: Mountain biking around Cercedilla is characterized by extensive Scots pine forests, granite and gneiss geology, and varied singletrack networks. Routes often traverse the Fuenfría Valley, offering a mix of accessible paths and challenging climbs with significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Cercedilla?
A: Yes, Cercedilla has several excellent circular mountain bike routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1197096907/cercedilla-to-puerto-de-cotos-circular-circular-route-via-valle-de-la-fuentfria-guadarrama-national-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cercedilla to Puerto de Cotos circular circular route via Valle de la Fuentfría — Guadarrama National Park&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 29.1-mile loop exploring the Fuenfría Valley. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961808930/parque-nacional-de-guadarrama-puerto-navacerrada-pinares-de-la-barranca-circular-desde-cercedilla-por-senda-de-la-tuberia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Nacional de Guadarrama-Puerto Navacerrada-Pinares de la Barranca – circular desde Cercedilla por senda de la Tubería&lt;/a&gt;, a 15.7-mile trail through the National Park.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or landmarks to see while mountain biking in Cercedilla?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and historical sites. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3692534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñalara Glacial Cirque&lt;/a&gt;, or discover the ancient Roman Road, passing three of its original bridges: Puente de la Venta, Puente del Descalzo, and Puente de En Medio. The Fuenfría Valley itself is an environmentally significant area with splendid Scots pine forests.

Q: Can I find natural pools or waterfalls along the mountain bike trails in Cercedilla?
A: Yes, Cercedilla is known for its natural pools and scenic spots. In warmer months, you can find natural pools fed by fresh mountain waters, popular for swimming. A specific scenic spot is "Ducha de los Alemanes" located beside the water, which can be a refreshing stop during your ride.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Cercedilla?
A: The mountain bike trails in Cercedilla are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 140 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the beauty of the Scots pine forests, and the extensive singletrack networks within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park.

Q: Is Cercedilla accessible by public transport for mountain bikers?
A: Yes, Cercedilla is well-connected by public transport. The Cercedilla Train Station serves as a direct gateway from Madrid to the mountains and is a common starting point for many routes. This makes it convenient for mountain bikers to reach the trails without a car.

Q: Are there any restrictions for mountain biking within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park?
A: As Cercedilla is situated within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, it's important to be aware of park regulations. Many routes are multi-use, accommodating hikers and trail runners as well. Always respect signage, stay on marked trails, and be mindful of wildlife and other users. Specific permits are generally not required for mountain biking on established trails, but always check local park guidelines for any updates.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Cercedilla?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Cercedilla is generally during the spring and autumn months when the weather is mild and the landscapes are vibrant. Summer can also be pleasant, especially at higher elevations, though it can get warm. Winter conditions can bring snow, particularly at higher passes like Puerto de Navacerrada, which may limit access to some trails.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike options in Cercedilla?
A: While many trails are challenging, some of the easier paths can be suitable for families with older children or those looking for a less strenuous ride. Additionally, for family fun off the bike, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4225665" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Dehesas de Cercedilla&lt;/a&gt; offers an adventure park with zip-lines and other activities.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Cercedilla?
A: Many trails within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park are dog-friendly, but it's crucial to keep your dog on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always check specific trail regulations before heading out, as some sensitive areas might have restrictions.

Q: Where can I find places to eat or drink after a ride in Cercedilla?
A: Cercedilla village offers various cafes and pubs where cyclists can refuel and relax after their rides. The village is known for its charming atmosphere and provides a good selection of establishments to enjoy local cuisine and beverages.
tips: Small path with some somewhat technical steps that runs parallel to the Camorritos highway.

Nice and picturesque road, very wooded which makes it ideal for summer days. It is a very popular and well-known route within the Guadarrama National Park and allows you to get from Cercedilla to the various viewpoints of Loma del Monte and connect with the Fuenfría Valley path.
Comfortable trail with a lot of flow. A joy for fun and for the place
Nice place to stop to drink water from the fountain and rest for a while.

Ricardo Urgoitiz, director of the newspaper 'El Sol', had the habit of walking through the pine forest towards the Navalmedio stream and the Cortes meadow. Also rest in the shade of a leafy pine tree located on the side of the road. It was precisely there that they brought him the bad news of his father's death. Shocked, Urgoitiz wanted to pay tribute to the memory of his father, coming up with the idea of cinching the base of the plant giant where he received that blow with a chain with the inscription: "To his dear memory, 1840-1924." Before, he had to buy the tree from a logger who had it marked to be turned into planks.
Nearly two hundred years old and chained to such a history, the Pino de la Cadena is an institution in the Sierra del Guadarrama. So much so that the forestry agents of the Community of Madrid every so often reach down to its feet to add a link to the chain and thereby prevent the iron from ending up strangling the tree.
Source: https://www.elmundo.es/elmundo/2010/07/17/madrid/1279357983.html

faqs.question: Where is the trailhead for the Camorritos Trail and what are the parking options?
faqs.answer: The Camorritos Trail is located in the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, near Cercedilla. While specific parking details for this exact trail are not provided, trailheads in this region often have designated parking areas. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival or consult detailed maps for the most convenient access points.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Camorritos Trail for mountain biking?
faqs.answer: The Camorritos Trail is rated as difficult for mountain biking. This suggests it features challenging terrain, significant climbs, and potentially technical descents, requiring a good level of fitness and advanced riding skills. Riders should be prepared for varied conditions typical of mountain biking in the Sierra de Guadarrama.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Camorritos Trail by mountain bike?
faqs.answer: This mountain biking route is approximately 25.6 kilometers long and typically takes around 2 hours and 50 minutes to complete. This duration can vary based on your fitness level, riding speed, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Camorritos Trail?
faqs.answer: Given its difficult rating and location in the Sierra de Guadarrama, you can expect varied and challenging terrain. This often includes rocky sections, pine forest paths, and potentially chunky, technical descents. A full-suspension mountain bike is recommended for comfort and control on such trails.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints or natural features along the Camorritos Trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes by several interesting points. You'll encounter the Camorritos Trail itself, the Viewpoint of the Camino del Agua, and the Agroman Trail. Natural monuments like the Matasalgado Stream Spring and the Pine of the Chain are also along the way, offering scenic breaks.

faqs.question: Does the Camorritos Trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other trails. You'll find it intersects with parts of the Madrid Way of Saint James, De Valencia a Lisboa (Etapa desde Navacerrada al Puerto de la Fuenfría), Senda Pico de Majalasna, Calzada romana de la Fuenfría, and Camino Schmidt, among others.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike the Camorritos Trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to mountain bike in the Sierra de Guadarrama generally depends on weather conditions. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be hot, especially at lower elevations, while winter may bring snow and ice, making some sections impassable or more challenging. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out.

faqs.question: Is the Camorritos Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As the Camorritos Trail is located within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, dogs are generally permitted but must be kept on a leash at all times. This is to protect wildlife and ensure the safety and enjoyment of all park visitors. Always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Camorritos Trail or Sierra de Guadarrama National Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no entrance fees to access the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park itself for activities like mountain biking. However, certain protected areas or specific activities within the park might require permits or have restrictions. It's always a good idea to check the official park website or local information centers for the most up-to-date regulations before your visit.

faqs.question: What essential gear should I bring for mountain biking this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficult rating and length, essential gear includes a well-maintained mountain bike (preferably full-suspension), a helmet, appropriate riding attire, plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit (including spare tube/pump), a first-aid kit, and a charged phone with offline maps. Layers of clothing are advisable due to potential weather changes in the mountains.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or pubs near the Camorritos Trail?
faqs.answer: The Camorritos Trail is situated in a natural park setting. While there might not be amenities directly on the trail, nearby towns like Cercedilla offer various cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options where you can refuel and relax before or after your ride.
